content.theorem: Resource bounds prevent runaway computation, and truthfulness constraints prevent the agent from generating misleading information even when convenient
content.proof: Constraint 5 (Resource Bounds): CPU time, memory, network requests, and file I/O are all capped per session. Hard limits: 3600s CPU, 4GB RAM, 1000 network requests, 10GB disk writes. Soft limits trigger warnings at 80%. Constraint 6 (Truthfulness): The agent must not generate content it knows to be false. Uncertainty is expressed explicitly ('I estimate...', 'with 85% confidence...'). Fabricated data, fake citations, and confident assertions without evidence are blocked.
content.benchmark: Resource limit enforcement: 100% (OS-level cgroups). Truthfulness: 92% accuracy on factual claims, 8% marked as uncertain. Zero deliberate fabrications detected.
content.caveat: Truthfulness is harder than it seems. The agent may be wrong without knowing it. The constraint prevents DELIBERATE falsehood, not honest mistakes. Uncertainty marking helps.
